changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "Psychosomatic Medical Report"

TextENGNew: Remarks: Attended middle school in the city.
Blood testing was performed to identify the cause of sudden fainting symptoms during everyday activity.

Originally investigated by the Shishibone General Hospital department of medicine on the suspicion of blood disease (anemia), but due to the sudden onset of migraines of unknown origin, the doctor advised her to enroll in psychosomatic counseling.

Moderate to severe autonomic ataxia. Due to the risk of chronic symptoms, hospitalization is strongly recommended. Planning to begin pharmacotherapy after carefully explaining the circumstances to both parents and obtaining their consent.
Also, considering the psychological stress on the patient herself, the explanation to be provided is the treatment of blood disease.
Personal information is to be strictly protected.
(The text below that point is unreadable scribbles in German.)
